questions.text: Pressure at a point inside a liquid does NOT depend on:
questions.explanation: Hydrostatic paradox: pressure depends only on depth h (P = ρgh).

questions.text: Pascal's Law states that pressure applied to an enclosed fluid is:
questions.explanation: Basis for hydraulic machines.

questions.text: Hydraulic lift works on the principle of:
questions.explanation: Force multiplication through fluid pressure.

questions.text: Which of the following remains constant in a streamline flow?
questions.explanation: In steady flow, velocity of every particle passing a fixed point is the same.

questions.text: The Equation of Continuity (Av = constant) is based on conservation of:
questions.explanation: Incompressible fluid mass entering must equal mass leaving.

questions.text: Bernoulli's Theorem is based on the law of conservation of:
questions.explanation: Sum of pressure energy, KE, and PE per unit volume is constant.

questions.text: According to Bernoulli's theorem, where velocity is high, pressure is:
questions.explanation: P + ½ρv² = constant. High v means low P.

questions.text: The SI unit of Coefficient of Viscosity is:
questions.explanation: Also called Poiseuille (Pl). 1 Pl = 10 Poise.

questions.text: Stokes' Law gives the viscous drag force as:
questions.explanation: Force on a sphere of radius r moving with velocity v.

questions.text: Terminal velocity is reached when:
questions.explanation: Weight is balanced by buoyancy and viscous drag.

questions.text: Reynolds Number (Re) determines the:
questions.explanation: Re < 1000 is streamline; Re > 2000 is turbulent.

questions.text: Surface Tension is defined as:
questions.explanation: T = F/L. It acts tangential to the surface.

questions.text: The angle of contact for a liquid that wets the surface (like water on glass) is:
questions.explanation: Adhesive forces are stronger than cohesive forces.

questions.text: Excess pressure inside a soap bubble is:
questions.explanation: A soap bubble has two surfaces (inner and outer).

questions.text: Rise of liquid in a capillary tube is given by:
questions.explanation: Known as Jurin's Law.

questions.text: With increase in temperature, viscosity of liquids _____ and viscosity of gases _____.
questions.explanation: In liquids, cohesion decreases; in gases, molecular collisions increase.

questions.text: Torricelli's Law relates the velocity of efflux to:
questions.explanation: v = sqrt(2gh), same as a free-falling body.

questions.text: Magnus Effect is an application of:
questions.explanation: Explains why a spinning ball curves in air.

questions.text: Venturimeter is used to measure:
questions.explanation: Works on Bernoulli's principle.

questions.text: Detergents reduce surface tension to:
questions.explanation: Lower surface tension allows water to penetrate small pores in clothes.

questions.text: Liquid drops are spherical due to:
questions.explanation: Surface tension tries to minimize surface area for a given volume.

questions.text: Archimedes' Principle states buoyant force equals:
questions.explanation: Basic principle of flotation.

questions.text: Hydraulic brakes work on:
questions.explanation: Equal pressure transmission to all wheels.

questions.text: The unit Poise is used for:
questions.explanation: CGS unit of viscosity.

questions.text: Excess pressure inside a liquid drop is:
questions.explanation: A drop has only one surface.

questions.text: Dimensions of Surface Tension are:
questions.explanation: Force [MLT⁻²] / Length [L].

questions.text: Atmospheric pressure is measured by:
questions.explanation: Mercury barometer was invented by Torricelli.

questions.text: 1 Atmospheric pressure is approximately:
Velocity of efflux depends on:
questions.explanation: v = sqrt(2gh).

questions.text: Viscosity is often called _____ in fluids.
questions.explanation: It resists relative motion between fluid layers.

questions.text: Blood pressure is measured using:
questions.explanation: Standard medical tool.

questions.text: In a horizontal pipe, where area is small, velocity is:
questions.explanation: Equation of continuity.

questions.text: The lift of an airplane wing is due to:
questions.explanation: Pressure difference between top and bottom.

questions.text: Surface tension vanishes at:
questions.explanation: Property of the interface disappears.

questions.text: Units of Surface Energy are:
questions.explanation: Energy per unit area (dimensionally same as Surface Tension).

questions.text: Ideal fluids are assumed to be:
questions.explanation: Simplification for Bernoulli's.

questions.text: Angle of contact for Mercury on glass is:
questions.explanation: Mercury does not wet glass; θ ≈ 135°.

questions.text: Viscous force depends on:
questions.explanation: F = ηA(dv/dx).

questions.text: A piece of ice floats in water. When it melts, the water level:
questions.explanation: Displaced water weight equals total ice weight.

questions.text: Units of Pressure are:
questions.explanation: Various valid units exist.
